CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Continued To May-25-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings On Plaintiff Andrew Catess Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ORI EDELSTEIN of The Ottinger Firm, P.C., Tel No. 415-463-1257 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JONATHAN M. BRENNER, ESQ. of Sidley Austin LLP, Tel No. 213-896-6634 appearing for Defendants KKR & Co. L.P. and KKR Credit Advisors (U.S.) LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling, and ordered as follows: DEFENDANT KKR & CO. L.P. AND KKR CREDIT ADVISORS (U.S.) L.L.C.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings On Plaintiff Andrew Catess Complaint is granted as to all claims, however labeled, regarding the equity awards and denied as to all other claims. Any dispute about whether a claim is or is not about an equity award is to be decided by the arbitrator. The parties' arbitration agreements covers all claims regarding the equity awards and those agreements are not substantively unconscionable and thus are enforceable. Plaintiff's initiation of arbitration proceeding regarding a bonus payment does not constitute a waiver of his right to object to the arbitration of his claims regarding a bonus payment. Under the facts of this case, where plaintiff has been requested to and has not yet paid a substantial arbitration fee and no meaningful proceedings have taken place in the arbitration matter, this court declines to follow the unpublished decision of Roderick v. Mazzetti & Associates (ND CA 2004) 2004 WL 2554453. Court shall stay the effect of its ruling to allow parties two months to file a writ. The stay would be in effect until such time as a writ filed within this two month period is ruled upon. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
